---
title: "TU/e Quarto Presentation Theme"
subtitle: "Reusable slide layout showcase"
author: "Ivo Roghair"
date: "18 June 2026"
format: tue-revealjs
tue-cover-image: assets/tue-bg-img1.jpg
tue-cover-logo: assets/tue-logo-white.png
# Red bar opacity (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque)
tue-cover-bar-opacity: 0.65
# Slide background colour — used for normal slides and the bottom footer strip of the cover slide
tue-slide-bg: "#f3efef" 
# Content slide text padding (top/bottom left/right), e.g. "30px 58px"
tue-content-slide-padding: "30px 58px"
# Short title shown on the left of the footer bar on every content slide
tue-short-title: "Ivo Roghair — TU/e Quarto Theme"
# Logo shown on the right of the footer bar (defaults to TU/e scarlet logo)
tue-footer-logo: "assets/tue-logo-wide-scarlet-alpha.png"
---

## Single Column Itemize

- Use this for agenda, framing, or key takeaways.
- Keep each bullet to one message.
- Use `**bold**` for emphasis and `==highlight==` for scanability.
- Replace all placeholder images at once by editing one line at the bottom:
	`[img-placeholder]: assets/rightpic.jpg`

$$
f(x) = \sum_{k=1}^{n} w_k\,\phi_k(x)
$$

## Two Columns Itemize

:::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Left Stream

- Problem context
- Constraints
- Required outcome
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Right Stream

- Candidate approach
- Risks
- Validation plan
:::
::::

## Single Image

![Placeholder image. Replace by editing `[img-placeholder]` once.][img-placeholder]{fig-alt="Placeholder image" width=92%}

## Two Images

:::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
![Left placeholder image][img-placeholder]{fig-alt="Left image" width=65%}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![Right placeholder image][img-placeholder]{fig-alt="Right image" width=65%}
:::
::::

## Itemize Plus Image

:::: columns
::: {.column width="56%"}
### Design Notes

- Left column for reasoning.
- Right column for visual evidence.
- Good for methods, workflows, and architecture.

$$
\mathbf{y}=\mathbf{A}\mathbf{x}+\mathbf{b}
$$
:::

::: {.column width="44%"}
![Supporting figure, placement towards top of slide][img-placeholder]{fig-alt="Supporting image" width=85% style="margin-top:-15%;"}
:::
::::

## Itemize Left, Large Image Right

:::: columns
::: {.column width="38%"}
### Insights

- Short bullets only.
- Keep visual dominant.
- Large image stays above footer region.
:::

::: {.column width="62%"}
![Large right-side image][img-placeholder]{fig-alt="Large right image" width=100%}
:::
::::

## Large Image Left, Itemize Right

:::: columns
::: {.column width="62%"}
![Large left-side image][img-placeholder]{fig-alt="Large left image" width=50%}
:::

::: {.column width="38%"}
### Commentary

- Mirror layout of previous slide.
- Useful for comparison pairs.
- Keep text density low.
:::
::::

## Equation Focus

Use this pattern for derivations or compact model summaries.

$$
\begin{aligned}
\nabla \cdot (\rho \mathbf{u}) &= 0 \\
\rho\left(\frac{\partial \mathbf{u}}{\partial t} + \mathbf{u}\cdot\nabla\mathbf{u}\right) &= -\nabla p + \mu\nabla^2\mathbf{u}
\end{aligned}
$$

## Highlight Box Pattern

<div style="background:#fff3bf;border-left:8px solid #c81818;padding:0.7rem 1rem;border-radius:8px;">
<strong>Highlight:</strong> This is a reusable callout style for decisions, warnings, and main conclusions.
</div>

- Add one callout per slide maximum.
- Use it to direct audience attention.

## Closing / Next Steps

1. Start from any slide pattern in this file.
2. Replace `[img-placeholder]` with your own image path once.
3. Duplicate and adapt layouts to build your final deck quickly.

## Python Code — Incremental Highlighting

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| code-line-numbers: "|1-2|4-7|9-12"
import numpy as np
from itertools import accumulate

# Build a Fibonacci growth sequence
seed = [1, 1]
fib = seed + [0] * 8
for i in range(2, 10):
    fib[i] = fib[i - 1] + fib[i - 2]

# Golden-ratio convergence
ratios = [fib[i + 1] / fib[i] for i in range(len(fib) - 1)]
print("Sequence:", fib)
print("Ratios  :", [f"{r:.4f}" for r in ratios])
print(f"φ ≈ {ratios[-1]:.6f}")
```

## Python Plot — Code & Output Side by Side

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| output-location: column-fragment
#| fig-format: svg
#| fig-width: 5.5
#| fig-height: 4.5
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

x = np.linspace(0, 4 * np.pi, 300)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5.5, 4.5),
                       facecolor='white')
ax.set_facecolor('#fafafa')

palette = ['#c81818', '#101073', '#00a2de']  # TU/e Scarlet, Deep Blue, Cyan
for a, c in zip([0.15, 0.4, 0.9], palette):
    y = np.sin(x) * np.exp(-a * x / (4 * np.pi))
    ax.plot(x, y, lw=2.4, color=c, label=f'α = {a}')

ax.fill_between(x, -0.3, 0.3,
                alpha=0.06, color='grey')
ax.set(xlabel='Phase (rad)',
       ylabel='Amplitude',
       title='Damped Oscillations')
ax.legend(frameon=True, fontsize=11)
ax.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.25)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Absolute Positioning — Drag-and-Drop Style

![](assets/application-x-addon-symbolic.svg){.absolute top=80 left=60 width="70"}

![](assets/application-x-addon-symbolic.svg){.absolute top=60 right=120 width="55"}

![](assets/application-x-addon-symbolic.svg){.absolute bottom=180 left=700 width="90"}

![](assets/application-x-addon-symbolic.svg){.absolute top=350 left=1200 width="50"}

::: {.absolute .tue-box-key top=120 left=200}
**Key Finding** — Agglomeration rates scale with the particle Reynolds number.
:::

::: {.absolute .tue-box-secondary top=350 left=80}
Secondary note placed freely on the canvas, just like a PowerPoint text box.
:::

::: {.absolute .tue-box-callout top=260 right=160}
Callout box — positioned wherever you need it on the slide.
:::

## Morph Demo — Square {auto-animate=true}

::: {.tue-morphbox .tue-morphbox--scarlet data-id="morphbox" style="width:220px;height:220px;"}
Step 1
:::

## Morph Demo — Pill {auto-animate=true}

::: {.tue-morphbox .tue-morphbox--pill .tue-morphbox--deep-blue data-id="morphbox" style="width:600px;height:120px;margin:140px auto;"}
Step 2 — wider
:::

## Morph Demo — Circle {auto-animate=true}

::: {.tue-morphbox .tue-morphbox--circle .tue-morphbox--green data-id="morphbox" style="width:320px;height:320px;margin:60px auto;"}
Step 3 — round
:::


## Callout boxes

You can still use the default quarto callouts (tip, warning, caution, note, etc)

::: {.callout-warning} 

### Practice makes perfect

Keep on practicing!

:::
